content:
I need to:
1. Read the compactor instructions from the prompt file
2. Read the current task_pending.md file
3. Apply the compaction rules carefully, preserving all critical information
4. Rewrite the file in place
5. Report the result in one line

Let me start by reading both files.
I'll read the compactor instructions and the task file to understand what needs to be compacted.

1	# task_pending.md Compactor
2	
3	You shrink `.archon/task_pending.md` in place, ahead of the plan agent. You do NOT change which targets are pending, add attempts/annotations, or migrate entries to `task_done.md`.
4	
5	## Scope
6	
7	- **Write target**: `.archon/task_pending.md` only.
8	- **Do not read or write** any other file in `.archon/`, any `.lean` file, `blueprint/**`, or `archon-protected.yaml`.
9	
10	## How to operate
11	
12	Default to **`Edit` calls**, one per compressible "Earlier attempts" entry. Each old multi-paragraph attempt becomes a single bullet via one `Edit` that replaces the paragraph with a one-line summary. This is faster and leaves the "Last attempt" / "Mathlib gaps" / "Notes" sections truly untouched.
13	
14	Use `Write` (full-file replace) only if the file's structure is so degraded that incremental edits would exceed ~15 calls.
15	
16	Do not compute the full rewrite mentally first — read once, then act incrementally.
17	
18	Reply with one line at the end:
19	- `COMPACTED: <pre-size> -> <post-size> chars` on success
20	- `UNCHANGED: <one-sentence reason>` if no rule applied (and call no tools)
21	
22	## Preservation rules (non-negotiable)
23	
24	The plan agent uses task_pending.md to know which targets are still open, what's been tried, and which Mathlib gaps block which targets. **Never drop or summarize away:**
25	
26	- Documented dead ends (`tried X, failed because Y`)
27	- Mathlib infrastructure gaps surfaced during attempts
28	- User hints / authorisations that landed in this file
29	- Cross-references to specific Mathlib declarations
30	- Any "next plan" suggestion an attempt left behind
31	
32	**When in doubt, KEEP.**
33	
34	## Per-entry compaction rules
35	
36	A pending entry typically looks like:
37	
38	```markdown
39	### `Foo.bar` (`Path/To/File.lean`)
40	- **Status**: open
41	- **Last attempt** (iter NNN): <multi-paragraph narrative>
42	- **Earlier attempts**:
43	  - iter MMM: <multi-paragraph narrative>
44	  - iter LLL: <multi-paragraph narrative>
45	- **Mathlib gaps**: ...
46	- **Notes**: ...
47	```
48	
49	Rewrite as:
50	
51	```markdown
52	### `Foo.bar` (`Path/To/File.lean`)
53	- **Status**: open
54	- **Last attempt** (iter NNN): <VERBATIM>
55	- **Earlier attempts**:
56	  - iter MMM: <one-sentence outcome>
57	  - iter LLL: <one-sentence outcome>
58	- **Mathlib gaps**: <VERBATIM>
59	- **Notes**: <VERBATIM>
60	```
61	
62	Rules:
63	
64	- **Last attempt**: keep verbatim — the plan agent will reason about it.
65	- **Earlier attempts**: each multi-paragraph entry becomes one line. Use one `Edit` per attempt (`old_string` = the paragraph, `new_string` = the bullet). Each one-liner MUST contain: **iteration number**, **approach name / short description**, **failure mode** (`Mathlib lacks <X>` / `proof obligation N reduces to false claim Y` / `prover stopped at sorry, no progress`). If three items in one sentence is awkward, use two sentences — never lose the failure mode.
66	- Do not merge attempts across iterations into a single line — each iteration's attempt is a separate dead end.
67	- If an entry has only one attempt: leave it untouched.
68	- If an entry has 5+ attempts: you may merge the **oldest two** into a range bullet `- iter LLL-MMM: <one sentence covering both>`, **only if** they failed for the same reason. If reasons differ, keep separate.
69	- "Mathlib gaps" / "Notes" / any user-hint-derived block: verbatim.
70	
71	## Anti-patterns
72	
73	- Delete a "Mathlib gap" block because the plan agent might "have noticed it elsewhere." Trust nothing — keep it.
74	- Migrate any entry to `task_done.md`. That is the plan agent's call after verification.
75	- Add `## Status: closed` annotations. Leave status fields as you found them.
76	- Invoke a subagent.
77	- Edit any other file.
78

1	# Index
2	<!-- One line per file. Update line numbers when the file changes. -->
3	
4	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — **closed iter-011** (see `task_done.md`). 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
5	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — **1 sorry**: `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L177). Status: **Phase C re-opened iter-076** per user directive. Closure routed through Steps C0→C3 in `STRATEGY.md`. File compiles. File LOC ~223.
6	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — **0 sorries** (closed iter-073). Status: DONE pending `sync_leanok`. File LOC 95. **Drop from current objectives.**
7	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean` — **closed iter-002**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
8	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean` — **closed iter-002**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE. **Forthcoming refactor (Phase C step C1, iter-079+):** body redefinition from `CommRing.Pic` proxy to invertible-$\mathcal O_X$-module; the protected signature stays.
9	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` — **closed iter-003**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
10	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — 1 sorry: `PicardFunctor.representable` (L190). Status: **Phase C re-opened iter-076**. Routed through Steps C0→C3. File compiles.
11	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` — **closed iter-004**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
12	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` — closed across iter-004/005/007/008. 0 sorries. Status: DONE. **Note for Phase C step C2 (iter-081+):** `etaleSheafified` needs re-derivation against refined `LineBundle`.
13	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` — 0 sorries; ~931 LOC. Status: DONE.
14	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` — **DELETED iter-063** (refactor subagent split). Replaced by:
15	  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietorisCore.lean` — 0 sorries; 629 LOC. Status: DONE.
16	  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietorisCover.lean` — 0 sorries; 713 LOC. Status: DONE.
17	  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — **6 syntactic sorries; 1270 LOC; FILE DOES NOT COMPILE**. Errors: L952 unsolved Module-instance equality; L1088 `simp made no progress`; L1170/1171 type mismatches in `h_diff_pi_smul_g` (`e₃` applied to wrong domain); L1182 + L1213 + L412 heartbeat timeouts (≥ 200000 heartbeats). **Iter-076 prover target: REPAIR — restore compilation while preserving the 6 residual sorries.**
18	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — **6 syntactic sorries; 823 LOC; FILE DOES NOT COMPILE**. Errors: L244 unsolved goal in `d_target.d`'s additivity; L248 rewrite-pattern mismatch (`CommRingCat.Hom.hom` resolution); L256 `ConcreteCategory.hom` not applied as function; L258 rewrite-pattern mismatch; L266 `rfl` claim fails for the adjunction-coherence identity; L297 `whnf` timeout in `cotangentExactSeqBeta`; L332 `No goals to be solved` (over-application); L342–L343 heartbeat timeouts; L381–L384 unknown identifier `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (cascade from upstream failure); L452 `simp made no progress`; L645 unknown identifier `cotangentExactSeq_structure`. **Iter-076 prover target: REPAIR — restore compilation; revert the in-flight Step 7 chain to clean sorries where it cannot be repaired in-iteration.**
19	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` — **does not exist yet**. To be scaffolded by the refactor subagent in iter-077 per Phase C step C0. Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex` (written this iter).
20	
21	## Recent prover dispatches (iter-067 onward)
22	
23	- **iter-070**: blueprint-only (user directive 2026-05-12). No prover dispatch.
24	- **iter-071** (executed): three parallel tracks. Net **26 → 19** (-7).
25	- **iter-072** (executed): four parallel lanes. Net **19 → 16** (-3).
26	- **iter-073** (executed): four parallel lanes. Net **16 → 15** (-1).
27	- **iter-074** (executed): two parallel lanes. Net **15 → 15** (no closures; structural progress only).
28	  - `Differentials.lean` 7 → 7. `_structure.h_zero` Steps 4–6 committed. ~147 LOC added.
29	  - `BasicOpenCech.lean` 6 → 6. `try`-wrapped `dsimp` chain + `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, …]` at L1077–L1086. ~109 LOC added.
30	- **iter-075** (executed): two parallel lanes. Net **15 → 15** (no closures; structural progress only).
31	  - `Differentials.lean` 7 → 7. `_structure.h_zero` Step 7 setup committed.
32	  - `BasicOpenCech.lean` 6 → 6. `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1128): dropped `try` wrappers; committed bare per-summand reduction.
33	- **Inter-iter (between iter-075 and iter-076 plan pass)**: prover work continued — Step 7 essentially complete inline in Differentials.lean (Steps 1–7 of `h_zero` written through to `hα_fac`/`hβ_fac` and `dsimp` collapses, ~660 LOC of cotangent infrastructure); per-summand chain in BasicOpenCech.lean further reduced via `rw [smul_comm]; congr 1` to the sign-free residual. **However**: both files no longer compile (see Index above). Net sorry count: **14** (Differentials 6 + BasicOpenCech 6 + Jacobian 1 + Picard/Functor 1 + AbelJacobi 0 + Genus 0).
34	
35	## Iter-076 plan — REPAIR ROUND + Phase C re-opening (strategic)
36	
37	**Two prover lanes** for repair. Both files must be made to compile again; new sorries are acceptable wherever an in-flight tactic chain cannot be repaired in-iteration. **No new feature work; no new helper lemmas; no chains of thin helpers**.
38	
39	In parallel, the plan agent re-opens Phase C at the strategic level (STRATEGY.md + blueprint chapter `Modules_Monoidal.tex` + softening of `Picard_Functor.tex`). The Lean-side Phase C scaffolding (new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean`) is deferred to iter-077, where it will be created via the refactor subagent.
40	
41	### Lane 1 — `Differentials.lean` (repair)
42	
43	**Goal.** Restore compilation. The current state has six "syntactic" sorries (L122, L604, L617, L662, L679, L821) but a half-formed `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` body (L237–L279) and `cotangentExactSeqBeta` body (L297–L361) and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` body (L385–L617) that all fail to elaborate.
44	
45	**Approach.** Repair the smallest-set-of-edits that restores `lake env lean` cleanness, preserving the residual sorries at L122 / L604 / L617 / L662 / L679 / L821. Where the in-flight body is salvageable, fix the specific compile error; where it would take a substantial re-derivation, **roll the body back to `sorry`** with a one-line comment naming the next-iteration entry point.
46	
47	**Specific errors to repair** (from `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass):
48	
49	1. **L244** — `d_target.d`'s additivity proof (`AddMonoidHom.mk'`'s `(by intro a b; simp)` argument) leaves an unsolved goal: `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a + b)) = D_X.d (... a) + D_X.d (... b)`. Suggested repair: replace `by intro a b; simp` with `by intro a b; rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_add, D_X.d_add]`.
50	
51	2. **L248** — `rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]` fails: the pattern `(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (f.c.app U)) (?a * ?b)` is not present in the current target. The `dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]` on L247 has already collapsed `AddMonoidHom.mk'.toFun` to `fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`, but the multiplication `(a * b)` may have a slightly different syntactic form. Suggested repair: `show D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a * b)) = ...; rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]; rfl` (give an explicit `show` to commit the LHS form).
52	
53	3. **L256** — `Function expected at ConcreteCategory.hom h, but this term has type ?m.364 ?m.372 ?m.373` — the `f.c.naturality f'` returns an equality, but the `congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x)` elaborates with `h` underspecified. Suggested repair: tag the type, `have := congr_arg (fun h : _ => (ConcreteCategory.hom h) x) (f.c.naturality f')`.
54	
55	4. **L258** — same pattern as L248: rewrite pattern not present. Suggested repair: insert an explicit `show ... = ...` before the `rw`.
56	
57	5. **L266** — `rfl` for `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit.app … ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'` fails. This is the adjunction-coherence identity. Likely it is NOT rfl — the LHS uses `φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback _ g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf` whereas the RHS involves `φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback _ (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf`, and the composition `(f ≫ g).base = g.base ≫ f.base` enters non-trivially. Suggested repair: this is a real lemma, not rfl. Roll back the `d_app` block to `sorry` with a comment naming the identity.
58	
59	6. **L297** — `whnf` timeout in `cotangentExactSeqBeta`. Possibly the `set adj_fg` / `set η` chain at L304–L306 is causing runaway elaboration. Suggested repair: simplify the body of `cotangentExactSeqBeta` by inlining `adj_fg.homEquiv` and `η` rather than introducing them as `let`s; or add `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000` at the top of the body if the chain is intrinsically heavy.
60	
61	7. **L332** — `No goals to be solved` at the `exact h4` step inside `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s `hη` proof. The `simp only` chain above closed the goal already, and the explicit `exact` then fires on an empty goal. Suggested repair: remove the redundant `exact h4`.
62	
63	8. **L342–L343** — heartbeat timeouts in `cotangentExactSeqBeta`. Same upstream issue as L297.
64	
65	9. **L381–L384** — `Unknown identifier cotangentExactSeqBeta` — cascade from the broken `cotangentExactSeqBeta` definition. Once L297 / L342 are repaired, this resolves automatically.
66	
67	10. **L452** — `simp made no progress`. In `cotangentExactSeq_structure`'s `h_zero` proof, the simp set `[SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` may now be a no-op after upstream simp lemmas reshape the goal. Suggested repair: replace with `dsimp only` or remove the line; or weaken to `try simp only [...]`.
68	
69	11. **L645** — `Unknown identifier cotangentExactSeq_structure` in `cotangent_exact_sequence` — cascade from the broken `cotangentExactSeq_structure` definition. Resolves once the upstream is repaired.
70	
71	**Fallback.** If a tactic cannot be repaired in the iteration, replace the surrounding `by`-block with `by sorry` and a `-- ITER-076: <one-line description of the issue>` comment. The **goal is compilation cleanness**, not closing more sorries.
72	
73	**Hard constraints**:
74	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
75	- **No new axioms.**
76	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies (user policy). Use `lean_diagnostic_messages` + `lean_goal` only.
77	- Sorry count budget: starts at 6; the repaired file should have at most **8** sorries (allow +2 if a complex sub-block has to be rolled back).
78	
79	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` (unchanged this iter).
80	
81	### Lane 2 — `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` (repair)
82	
83	**Goal.** Restore compilation. Six "syntactic" sorries (L495, L819, L847, L1166, L1181, L1260), but the in-flight `h_diff_pi_smul_g` (L1167–L1181) and the smul-transport in `f_R.map_smul'` / `g_R.map_smul'` (L1193–L1231) have type errors and timeouts.
84	
85	**Specific errors to repair** (from `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass):
86	
87	1. **L952** — unsolved goal: `Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k …) = Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`. The proof of `h_mod_pi₃` (deriving the `R`-module instance on `∏ᶜ Z₃` from `Pi.module`) leaves a residual equality of instances. Suggested repair: use `convert h_mod_pi₃` or `change Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃); exact h_mod_pi₃` to bridge the syntactic gap.
88	
89	2. **L1088** — `simp made no progress`. In `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, after the bulk `simp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, …]` (L1080–L1087), the next `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum]` (L1088) fires on a target that is already in the desired form (or the lemmas don't fire for some other reason). Suggested repair: replace `simp only` with `try simp only` to avoid the failure, or drop the line entirely.
90	
91	3. **L1170 / L1171** — type mismatch in `h_diff_pi_smul_g`: `(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • y))` has type `↑scK₀.X₃`, but `e₃` expects `↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`. Suggested repair: the prover added `h_diff_pi_smul_g` in the same shape as `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, but the codomain instance is wrong — `e₃` is on `∏ᶜ Z₃` not `scK₀.X₃`. The intended statement is via `e₃`, with `scK₀.g`'s codomain matched through `convert`. Either re-statement of the type, or insert appropriate `change` / `convert`. If non-trivial, simply roll back `h_diff_pi_smul_g`'s body to `sorry` (preserving the `letI`s and the bare statement).
92	
93	4. **L1182** — `isDefEq` heartbeat timeout in `h_diff_pi_smul_g`'s `try` chain. Suggested repair: remove the `try funext j` / `try simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` chain on L1179–L1180, leaving just `intro r y; sorry`.
94	
95	5. **L1205** — unsolved goals (the `congr 1` step inside `f_R.map_smul'` produces a residual equality `(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (r • x) = (ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • e₁ x))`). The original chain L1194–L1214 has been corrupted by recent edits. Suggested repair: verify the `calc` chain step-by-step; the missing piece is the `Equiv.smul_def`-based reduction. If too intricate, roll back `f_R.map_smul'`'s body to `sorry` and similarly `g_R.map_smul'`.
96	
97	6. **L1213** — `whnf` heartbeat timeout. Same root cause as L1205 (the `LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply` rewrite at L1214).
98	
99	7. **L412** — `whnf` timeout in some upstream substep. May be unrelated to the smul transport. Investigate (read context around L412); if it's in the `h_a` / `h_a_fun` setup of one of the dead-end substeps (L495 territory), wrap in `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in` locally.
100	
101	**Fallback.** Same as Lane 1: replace problematic `by`-blocks with `by sorry` and a `-- ITER-076` comment.
102	
103	**Hard constraints**:
104	- **No new project-local helper lemmas.**
105	- **No new axioms.**
106	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
107	- Sorry count budget: starts at 6; the repaired file should have at most **8** sorries.
108	
109	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers* (unchanged this iter); also `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`.
110	
111	## Off-limits this iteration
112	
113	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L177) — Phase C now active but the Lean-side closure is iter-085+ at the earliest.
114	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` (0 sorries) — DONE iter-073.
115	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` `representable` — gated on C0–C3.
116	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` (0 sorries) — DONE iter-011.
117	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean` (0 sorries) — DONE iter-002. Refactor C1 deferred to iter-079+.
118	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` (0 sorries) — DONE. C2 re-derivation deferred to iter-081+.
119	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` — does not exist; refactor subagent in iter-077.
120	- **No new feature work in either lane this iteration.** The lanes are repair-only.
121	
122	## Build environment note (CORRECTED iter-076)
123	
124	The persistent "Build environment broken since iter-069" note in PROGRESS.md / `task_pending.md` was **stale**. `lean_diagnostic_messages` works correctly and returns useful errors; `lean_leansearch` works; the LSP responds. What was actually broken is the *project source*: in-flight prover bodies in `Differentials.lean` and `BasicOpenCech.lean` have accumulated type errors and heartbeat timeouts across iter-074/075. The "env broken" framing led the iter-074 and iter-075 prover dispatches to "commit credible tactics without LSP verification" — which is what produced the current broken state.
125	
126	**This iteration corrects the framing and the policy.** The build env works. The iter-076 prover lanes use `lean_diagnostic_messages` to verify each tactic-edit before committing. The "no `lean_run_code` pre-validation" constraint stays in place (user policy), but `lean_diagnostic_messages` is *not* pre-validation of candidate bodies — it's verification of committed-to-disk edits, which is the standard development feedback loop.
127	
128	## Earlier CLOSED plan summaries (iter-061 through iter-075)
129	
130	- **iter-075** (executed): 2-lane prover round; net 15 → 15.
131	- **iter-074** (executed): 2-lane round; net 15 → 15.
132	- **iter-073** (executed): 4-lane round; net 16 → 15.
133	- **iter-072** (executed): 4-lane round; net 19 → 16.
134	- **iter-071** (executed): 3-lane round; net 26 → 19.
135	- **iter-069**: three-track round; closed 4 substeps in BasicOpenCech; `cotangentExactSeqBeta`; +169 LOC.
136	- **iter-068, iter-067**: No-op iterations.
137	- **iter-066, iter-065, iter-064, iter-063, iter-062, iter-061**: Earlier Phase A/B progress.
138	
139	## Forward scoping (post iter-076)
140	
141	**Iter-077 (post-repair)**:
142	- Resume narrowing on Differentials.lean: `_structure.h_zero` should be closable inline post-repair (the inflight chain at L440+ is salvageable if the upstream `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` body compiles).
143	- Resume narrowing on BasicOpenCech.lean: `h_diff_pi_smul_f` per-summand chain (i)–(iv) close, then `h_diff_pi_smul_g` shifted-degree close.
144	- Invoke **refactor subagent** to scaffold `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` per Phase C step C0 directive (blueprint chapter `Modules_Monoidal.tex` is the input).
145

147	- Differentials `_structure.h_exact` (L604) — route (a) project-local `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise`.
148	- Differentials `_structure.h_epi` (L617) — `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` + `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` + project-local `SheafOfModules.epi_iff_epi_presheaf`.

154	- Phase C step C2 — refactor `Picard/Functor.lean`, re-derive `PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified`.

157	- Phase C step C3 (longest leg) — construct $\Pic_{C/k}$.
158	
159	## Helper targets seeded by iter 001 / 002 / 003 / 004 / 005 (compact index)
160	
161	All seeded helpers are closed; preserved here as a compact dependency reference (full detail in `task_done.md`).
162

164
